title: गणेश स्तुति (मुद्गल पुराण)
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गणेश स्तुति - मुद्गल पुराण ॥

ॐ गणानां त्वा गणपतिं हवामहे
कविं कवीनामुपमश्रवस्तमम्।
ज्येष्ठराजं ब्रह्मणां ब्रह्मणस्पत
आ नः शृण्वन्नूतिभिः सीद सादनम्॥१॥

प्रणम्य शिरसा देवं गौरीपुत्रं विनायकम्।
भक्तावासं स्मरेन्नित्यमायुष्कामार्थसिद्धये॥२॥

वक्रतुण्ड महाकाय सूर्यकोटिसमप्रभ।
निर्विघ्नं कुरु मे देव सर्वकार्येषु सर्वदा॥३॥

मुद्गले पुराणे यः स्तुतो गणनायकः।
सिद्धिबुद्धिसमायुक्तः पार्वतीनन्दनोऽव्ययः॥४॥

अष्टौ रूपाणि यस्यैव गणेशस्य महात्मनः।
वक्रतुण्डश्चैकदन्तो महोदरो गजाननः॥५॥

लम्बोदरो विकटश्च विघ्नराजो धूम्रवर्णकः।
एतान्यष्टौ स्वरूपाणि मुद्गले प्रकटानि हि॥६॥

मोदकप्रियहस्तं तं मूषकवाहनमीश्वरम्।
सिन्दूरवर्णं गणपं मुद्गलोक्तं भजाम्यहम्॥७॥

सर्वविघ्नहरं देवं सर्वविघ्नविवर्जितम्।
सर्वसिद्धिप्रदातारं वन्दे गणपतिं प्रभुम्॥८॥

॥ इति श्री मुद्गल पुराणोक्त गणेश स्तुति सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ganesha Stuti - Mudgala Purana ||

Om Gananam tva Ganapatim havamahe
Kavim kavinam upama-shravastamam.
Jyeshtha-rajam Brahmanam Brahmanaspata
A nah shrinvann utibhih sida sadanam.||1||

Pranamya shirasa devam Gauri-putram Vinayakam.
Bhaktavasam smaren-nityam ayush-kamartha-siddhaye.||2||

Vakratunda Mahakaya Surya-koti-samaprabha.
Nirvighnam kuru me deva sarva-karyeshu sarvada.||3||

Mudgale Purane yah stuto Gana-nayakah.
Siddhi-Buddhi-samayuktah Parvati-nandano'vyayah.||4||

Ashtau rupani yasyaiva Ganeshaya Mahatmanah.
Vakratundash-chaikadanto Mahodaro Gajananah.||5||

Lambodaro Vikatash-cha Vighnarajo Dhumravarnakah.
Etany-ashtau swarupani Mudgale prakatani hi.||6||

Modaka-priya-hastam tam Mushaka-vahanam-Ishwaram.
Sindura-varnam Ganapam Mudgaloktam bhajamy-aham.||7||

Sarva-vighna-haram Devam Sarva-vighna-vivarjitam.
Sarva-siddhi-pradataram Vande Ganapatim Prabhum.||8||

|| Iti Shri Mudgala Puranoktam Ganesha Stuti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: मुद्गल पुराण में वर्णित गणेश स्तुति में गणपति के आठ रूपों का वर्णन है - वक्रतुण्ड, एकदन्त, महोदर, गजानन, लम्बोदर, विकट, विघ्नराज और धूम्रवर्ण। गणों के अधिपति, कवियों में श्रेष्ठ, ज्येष्ठ, ब्रह्मणस्पति गणपति का आवाहन किया जाता है। सिद्धि-बुद्धि सहित, पार्वतीनन्दन, मोदकप्रिय, मूषकवाहन, सिन्दूरवर्ण गणपति की स्तुति से सर्व विघ्न दूर होते हैं।
benefits: Invokes all eight forms of Ganesha from Mudgala Purana
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles in spiritual and worldly endeavors
Ancient Vedic prayer with immense spiritual potency
Traditionally recited to invoke long life, fulfillment of desires and achievement
description: Ganesha Stuti - Mudgala Purana — गणेश स्तुति (मुद्गल पुराण) — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Ganesha. Attributed to Mudgala Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to invoke all eight forms of Ganesha from Mudgala Purana, ensure long life, fulfillment of desires and achievement, and remove all obstacles in spiritual and worldly endeavors. It is especially recommended on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Ganesh Chaturthi for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 7 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Ganesha Stuti - Mudgala Purana in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ganesha Stuti from Mudgala Purana in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Ancient prayer describing eight forms of Ganesha for obstacle removal.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ganesha Stuti from the Mudgala Purana?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganesha Stuti — Mudgala Purana (गणेश स्तुति - मुद्गल पुराण) is an 8-verse Sanskrit hymn praising Bhagavan Ganesha through His ashta-rupa (eight cosmic forms) as enumerated in the Mudgala Purana — one of the two principal upapuranas of the Ganapatya Sampradaya (the other being the Ganesha Purana). The stuti opens with the Vedic Ganapati Sukta (Om Gananam tva Ganapatim havamahe — RV 2.23.1), proceeds through the universally chanted Pranamya shirasa devam Gauri-putram Vinayakam and Vakratunda Mahakaya Surya-koti samaprabha invocation shlokas, and then enumerates the eight Mudgala Purana forms — Vakratunda, Ekadanta, Mahodara, Gajanana, Lambodara, Vikata, Vighnaraja and Dhumravarna — concluding with Sarva-vighna-haram devam... vande Ganapatim prabhum. (Source: Mudgala Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Mudgala Purana and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mudgala Purana (मुद्गल पुराण) is one of the two principal upapuranas of the Ganapatya Sampradaya (Ganesha-worshipping tradition), composed by Sage Mudgala — the rishi-kulapati who is said to be the same Mudgala-rishi mentioned in the Rigveda. The text is structured as a dialogue between Mudgala and Daksha-prajapati expounding the eight cosmic forms (ashta-rupa) of Ganesha — each form having descended in a specific yuga to slay a particular asura representing one of the eight major doshas (defects) of the human personality: Matsara (jealousy), Mada (arrogance), Moha (delusion), Lobha (greed), Krodha (anger), Kama (lust), Mamta (possessiveness) and Ahamkara (ego). The Mudgala Purana is the foundational text for Ganapatya philosophy alongside the Ganesha Purana and the Ganapati Atharvashirsha. (Sources: Mudgala Purana opening; Ganapatya Sampradaya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the eight forms (ashta-rupa) of Ganesha in the Mudgala Purana?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mudgala Purana enumerates Ganesha's ashta-avatara — each form descending in a specific yuga to destroy a specific asura: (1) Vakratunda (curved-trunk) — slayer of Matsarasura (jealousy-demon) — vahana lion; (2) Ekadanta (single-tusked) — slayer of Madasura (arrogance-demon) — vahana mouse; (3) Mahodara (great-bellied) — slayer of Mohasura (delusion-demon) — vahana mouse; (4) Gajanana (elephant-faced) — slayer of Lobhasura (greed-demon) — vahana mouse; (5) Lambodara (long-bellied) — slayer of Krodhasura (anger-demon) — vahana mouse; (6) Vikata (extraordinary-formed) — slayer of Kamasura (lust-demon) — vahana peacock; (7) Vighnaraja (Lord of obstacles) — slayer of Mamatasura (possessiveness-demon) — vahana Shesha-naga; (8) Dhumravarna (smoke-coloured) — slayer of Ahamkarasura (ego-demon) — vahana mouse. Each verse 5-6 of the stuti enumerates these eight names. (Source: Mudgala Purana ashta-avatara doctrine.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the famous Vakratunda Mahakaya Suryakoti Samaprabha?
seoFaqs.answer: The shloka — Vakratunda Mahakaya Surya-koti samaprabha, Nirvighnam kuru me deva sarva-karyeshu sarvada — translates as: O Vakratunda (curved-trunked Lord), of mahakaya (great body), of Surya-koti samaprabha (splendor equal to ten million suns), Deva (divine one), make all my undertakings (sarva-karyeshu) free of obstacles (nirvighnam) at all times (sarvada). This is the single most-recited Ganesha invocation across India — chanted before every puja, before any new venture, at the start of every day, and as the dhyana-shloka before any spiritual practice. The shloka encapsulates Ganesha as Vighna-harta (obstacle-remover), Mahakaya (cosmic-bodied), Surya-koti-prabha (infinite-radiant), and the deva who grants nirvighna-siddhi (obstacle-free success) in sarva-karya (every undertaking). The verse appears here as the third verse of the Mudgala Purana stuti. (Sources: Mudgala Purana; classical Ganesha invocation.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Ganesha Stuti from Mudgala Purana be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: This stuti is most powerful on Wednesday (Budhvar — the day of Ganesha and Buddha-graha), Sankashti Chaturthi (Krishna paksha fourth — most powerful monthly Ganesha-day), Vinayaka Chaturthi (Shukla paksha fourth), Ganesh Chaturthi / Vinayaka Chavithi (the 10-day Ganesh Utsav of Bhadrapad Shukla Chaturthi to Anant Chaturdashi), Magh Shukla Chaturthi (Ganesh Jayanti), Angarika Chaturthi (Tuesday-Sankashti — most powerful obstacle-removal day), at the start of every puja as the prathama-pujya invocation, before any new venture (gruha-pravesh, vyavasaya, education), as a daily morning chant, and during the Ashta-Vinayaka yatra in Maharashtra (the eight ancient Ganesha temples of Maharashtra — Moreshwar Morgaon, Siddhi-Vinayak Siddhatek, Ballaleshwar Pali, Varada-Vinayak Mahad, Chintamani Theur, Girijatmaja Lenyadri, Vighneshvar Ozar, Mahaganapati Ranjangaon). (Sources: Mudgala Purana paddhati; Ganesha upasana calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Mudgala Purana stuti differ from Ganesha Atharvashirsha and Ganesha Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are foundational Ganapatya texts but differ in scope and emphasis. (1) The Ganapati Atharvashirsha (Atharvaveda Upanishad) is a 10-verse Vedic Upanishad establishing Ganesha as Para-Brahma (Tvameva pratyaksham tattvam-asi) — the supreme reality identified with Om — recited as a Vedic mantra-pada with svara, used for Ganesha homa and abhisheka. (2) The Ganesha Sahasranama (Ganesha Purana) is a 1000-name encyclopedic chant cataloging every aspect, form, leela and quality of Ganesha — used for major puja and homa. (3) The Mudgala Purana Stuti (8 verses) is uniquely focused on the ashta-avatara doctrine — connecting each Ganesha form to a specific human-defect-destroying mission, providing the philosophical framework of Ganesha as the destroyer of the eight inner asuras (Matsara, Mada, Moha, Lobha, Krodha, Kama, Mamta, Ahamkara). Devotees recite all three in major Ganapatya anushthana. (Sources: Ganapatya Sampradaya texts; comparative Ganesha stotra literature.)
